goal: build a well-tested CLI tool published to npm

causalExpectations.causation: TDD causes test files to exist for every CLI command before those commands are implemented, which this goal requires by definition because the goal explicitly names being well-tested as a primary quality property — TDD is the only practice that structurally guarantees tests exist for every command path rather than being added opportunistically after shipping

Documentation causes the CLI to have a README with installation instructions, usage examples, and flag descriptions, which a published npm package requires because packages without documentation have zero adoption — a user encountering the package on npm for the first time has no path to first use without docs, making the publish step pointless

TypeScript review causes the CLI argument parsing, option types, and exported utilities to be type-safe, which a published npm CLI requires because TypeScript type errors in published packages cause integration failures in consumers' TypeScript projects — typed exports are part of the published surface area that cannot be patched without a new release

Code review causes the CLI error handling, exit codes, and cross-platform path handling to be validated, which a published npm tool requires because CLI tools run in diverse environments — missing error handling produces unhandled promise rejections, wrong exit codes break CI pipelines that consume the tool as a build step

Planning causes CLI development to be sequenced with argument schema designed before command implementations, tests written before publishing, and documentation completed before release, which the npm publish goal requires because a published package cannot be cleanly unpublished and a broken first release permanently damages the package reputation on the registry

expectedOutcome: A cross-platform CLI tool published to npm with semantic versioning, comprehensive test coverage on all command paths, typed exports, graceful error messages, and a README with installation and usage examples
